message:
[medium] intercom: reject base_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

IntercomConfig::from_params stored params["base_url"] verbatim with no
URL-shape validation. base_url_policy was only consulted in
provisioning_readiness (advisory — it does not gate handle_configure).
A configure payload like

    {"access_token": "t", "base_url": "https://api.intercom.io?leak=x"}

would pass from_params and flow into IntercomClient::new where it is
concatenated via format!("{}{path}", self.base_url) in every request
method (client.rs:158/176/188). `?leak=x` would leak on every Intercom
API request with the endpoint path parsed as part of the query.
Userinfo (`https://attacker:pw@api.intercom.io`) would bake into every
request URL and silently override the Authorization header.

- New reject_base_url_qfu helper called from IntercomConfig::from_params
  that parses the URL and rejects userinfo (username or password) and
  query/fragment components at configure time. Host-allowlist advisory
  in base_url_policy is preserved unchanged.
- Five new unit tests: accept clean api.intercom.io, reject query /
  fragment / userinfo, reject unparseable URL.

Verification: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cargo check -p
fcp-intercom --tests is still blocked by the upstream asupersync main
E0252 regression at src/runtime/waker.rs:128. Intercom diff reviewed
manually: additions use std url::Url accessor calls with FcpError
shapes identical to the surrounding base_url_policy path.
reject_base_url_qfu mirrors the helper added in the bitbucket patch
d524224a this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
